broker_fee_detail: No-fee under FARE Act; listed by AM Residential
broker_message: Hi George — I'm interested in 353 Bay Ridge Pkwy #1. Before applying, could you confirm total move-in costs, that there's no broker fee, and the exact stove install date? Given it's not in yet and it's a ground-floor unit, would the owner consider $1,600 or a partial free-month? I'd also like to confirm rent-stabilization. Thanks!
broker_questions: What floor/direction does Apt 1 face — street or courtyard?
When exactly will the stove be installed (in writing)?
Is it rent-stabilized? Can I see the DHCR rent history and legal rent?
How reliable is heat/hot water in winter?
Status of the 2022 DOB chimney vacate order?
building_risk_rationale: Total HPD violations (~12) sit well below the 0.81/unit benchmark and there are no bedbugs/evictions, but elevated 311 (42 vs ~17 nabe avg), 10 DOB complaints, 9 ECB penalties incl. a $6,250 active fine, a recurring heat/hot-water pattern (2024-2026), an active 2022 DOB Partial Vacate Order at the chimney/courtyard, and ownership linked to Michael Niamonitakis (worst-landlord watchlist) push it above 'low.' Exact open count inconsistent (openigloo ~2, augrented '16'), unverified.
commute_notes: Nearest is the R local at 77th St, one block away — slow local, ~50-60 min to Midtown. Transfer to the N express at 59th St for a faster ride.
concessions_to_ask: free/reduced first month given no stove
confirm no broker fee in writing
cons: Ground-floor unit with incomplete kitchen (no stove yet)
Recurring heat/hot-water complaints and unresolved chimney vacate order
Owner tied to worst-landlord watchlists; slow R-only commute
leverage_notes: Rent already at low end of Bay Ridge studio range and below 2025 asking; modest leverage from incomplete kitchen, ground-floor, heat history — push for a small cut or concession, not a big drop.
noise_risks: ground-floor unit exposed to street/sidewalk noise
elevator/building mechanicals on a low floor
owner_name: Michael Niamonitakis (associated principal; flagged on NYC watchlists)
plain_english: A fairly-priced, no-fee Bay Ridge studio in an elevator/laundry building one block from the R — a reasonable deal at $1,695, below the neighborhood median. Catches: ground-floor, stove not installed yet, recurring winter heat history, and an owner linked to worst-landlord watchlists. Just listed (not sitting). Worth touring: verify light and kitchen in person, confirm heat reliability and stabilization, and try for $1,600 or a concession.
pros: No fee and rent below the Bay Ridge studio median and its own 2025 asking
Elevator + laundry + live-in super, R one block away
Fresh listing (1 day), priced to move

red_flags.how_to_verify: Tour; check window exposure and direction.
red_flags.why: More street noise, less light, higher security/pest exposure; only 4 photos.

red_flags.how_to_verify: Get install date in writing before signing.
Ask super about winter heat; check HPD Online.
red_flags.issue: Recurring building heat/hot-water complaints + active chimney vacate order
red_flags.why: Multiple 2024-2026 heat emergencies and an unresolved 2022 DOB Partial Vacate Order.

red_flags.how_to_verify: Search entity on JustFix/WhoOwnsWhat; ask current tenants.
red_flags.issue: Ownership tied to NYC worst-landlord watchlists

unverified: exact live HPD open count (aggregators inconsistent ~2 vs 16)
